title: podman-load not tagging image as documented in man page
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

podman load does not behave as documented.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Take existing image such as example:1, save to image as something like "podman -o image.tar example:1".

2. Delete the image such as "podman rmi example:1

3. Load the image as "podman load -i image.tar example:1

**Describe the results you received:**

Image is loaded without being tagged.

**Describe the results you expected:**

"man podman-load" documents the command with "podman load [OPTIONS] NAME[:TAG|@DIGEST]". It should have essentially tagged the image as the command "podman tag id example:1" would do.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:       1.0.0
Go Version:    go1.11.4
Git Commit:    "49780a1cf10d572edc4e1ea3b8a8429ce391d47d"
Built:         Tue Jan 15 03:38:17 2019
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```

    version: 'conmon version 1.12.0-dev, commit: 49780a1cf10d572edc4e1ea3b8a8429ce391d47d'

    version: "29"
  MemFree: 2177036288
  MemTotal: 33742360576
  OCIRuntime:
    package: runc-1.0.0-66.dev.gitbbb17ef.fc29.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: |-
      runc version 1.0.0-rc6+dev
      commit: ead425507b6ba28278ef71ad06582df97f2d5b5f
      spec: 1.0.1-dev
  SwapFree: 16580866048
  SwapTotal: 16852709376
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 16
  hostname: desk.mcneil.com
  kernel: 4.19.15-300.fc29.x86_64
  os: linux
  rootless: true
  uptime: 118h 57m 55.37s (Approximately 4.92 days)

  - docker.io

  ConfigFile: /home/sean/.config/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 1
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions:
  - overlay.mount_program=/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs
  GraphRoot: /home/sean/.local/share/containers/storage

    Backing Filesystem: extfs

    Supports d_type: "true"
  ImageStore:

  RunRoot: /run/user/1000
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body:
I'm having the same issue, however when I take a look at the generated tar ( as a result of  
podman save -o flasko-v1.0.tar  flasko:v1.0 ), I don't see anything mentioning the tag v1.0
Looks to work for :latest, but other :tags not so much.

Ooops, I misread my images output, looks to be working for me or is my below test methodology off?  (Note: I removed a lot of output below for clarity)

```
# podman -v
podman version 1.0.1-dev
# podman pull alpine

# podman tag alpine alpine:1

# podman save -o alpine.tar alpine:1
# podman rmi -a -f

# podman load -i alpine.tar alpine:1
# podman images
REPOSITORY         TAG   IMAGE ID       CREATED       SIZE
localhost/alpine   1     3f53bb00af94   5 weeks ago   4.67 MB
```
@seanmcneil3 @carroarmato0 What version of podman are you seeing this with?

It would appear it is not related to podman command as it is fixed without that package changing. runc, however, has been updated:

from

  OCIRuntime:
    package: runc-1.0.0-66.dev.gitbbb17ef.fc29.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: |-
      runc version 1.0.0-rc6+dev
      commit: ead425507b6ba28278ef71ad06582df97f2d5b5f

to

 OCIRuntime:
    package: runc-1.0.0-68.dev.git6635b4f.fc29.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: |-
      runc version 1.0.0-rc6+dev
      commit: ef9132178ccc3d2775d4fb51f1e431f30cac1398-dirty
